trade.summary: Arabia Saudita is India's third-largest trade partner and second-largest source of crude oil. India imports crude oil, LPG, and petrochemicals. India exports engineering goods, chemicals, pharmaceuticals, food products, and textiles. Arabia Saudita is also a major destination for Indian worker remittances.

diplomatic.summary: India and Arabia Saudita have a 'Strategic Partnership'. Crown Prince MBS and PM Modi have met multiple times. Arabia Saudita hosts the world's second-largest Indian diaspora (~2.4 million). Remittances from Saudi-based Indian workers are significant. The relationship is balanced between economic interdependence and India's growing ties with Saudi rivals Iran and Israele. India supports Gulf stability and is developing the India-Middle East-Europe economic corridor (IMEC) which passes through Arabia Saudita.

military.summary: Growing defence cooperation. Joint exercises (Al Mohed Al Hindi naval exercises). India supplies some defence products to Arabia Saudita. Cooperation on counter-terrorism and maritime security in the Arabian Sea.

financial.summary: Arabia Saudita's Public Investment Fund (PIF) has invested significantly in India across technology, energy, and infrastructure. Saudi Aramco has explored equity stakes in Indian refineries. Indian diaspora remittances from Arabia Saudita exceed $12 billion annually. Saudi petrochemical companies invest in Indian downstream.

energy.summary: Arabia Saudita is India's second-largest crude oil supplier (about 15-18% of imports). Saudi Aramco is a major strategic partner for India's refinery expansion. Cooperation on strategic petroleum reserves and LPG supply is ongoing. India and Arabia Saudita are working on a green hydrogen corridor.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation. Indian engineers and scientists work in Saudi Aramco and SABIC. Joint research in renewable energy (especially solar) and desalination technology. Saudi universities host Indian faculty.
